main_event: Jacksonville, Florida civilian Toni Veverka reported seeing three unidentified objects at 15-minute intervals at 5 AM on July 11, 1950; a July 14 newspaper account reports approximately 100 courthouse observers watching a balloon-like shiny object; file consists entirely of newspaper clippings transmitted by HQ Third Army G-2.
sighted_object: three objects at 15-minute intervals (Veverka, July 11); balloon-like shiny object (courthouse crowd, July 14)
witness_description: Primary witness: Toni Veverka, Jacksonville resident (July 11 sighting, 3 objects at 15-min intervals at 5 AM); secondary event: approximately 100 persons at courthouse (July 14, balloon-like shiny object)
interesting_points: File contains no ATIC Form 329 record card — consists entirely of 3 newspaper clippings and 1 HQ Third Army G-2 transmittal letter
First newspaper clipping (Florida Times-Union July 11) is missing from file — page 1 absent
Three separate newspaper accounts: FL Times-Union Jul 11 (Veverka sighting), Miami Herald Jul 13, FL Times-Union Jul 14 (courthouse crowd)
July 14 clipping describes ~100 courthouse observers — likely separate event from Veverka's July 11 sighting
No ATIC investigation, no formal conclusion; purely newspaper-clip intelligence report
CSV witness count flag: '100 courthouse observers' appears in Jul 14 clipping but is a different event from the primary Veverka sighting
